name: A beam fell on me at a Boise construction site—do I need a lawyer?
description: Yes for third-party claims; they investigate and maximize recovery.

mainEntity.name: A beam fell on me at a Boise construction site—do I need a lawyer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes for third-party claims; they investigate and maximize recovery.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Yes, hire a construction injury lawyer, especially for third-party claims beyond workers' comp in Idaho.** Multiple parties like subs, owners, or manufacturers may be liable, and lawyers investigate fault[2]. **Key factors:** 1. Workers' comp is no-fault but limited. 2. Third-party suits need proof of negligence under Idaho Code §72-223. 3. 2-year statute of limitations. 4. Comparative fault reduces recovery if partly your fault[2]. Don't sign insurer statements alone; a lawyer identifies all claims and negotiates better settlements. Most work on contingency—no win, no fee.
